title: AI Traffic Companion — iPhone passenger-seat AI for solo drivers (CarBUDDY realized)
html:
AI Traffic Companion — iPhone passenger-seat AI for solo drivers (CarBUDDY realized)

Source: 2025-08-13 Google Doc. The CarBUDDY idea from 2024 12 28 ideas list gets a full architectural write-up — voice-first iPhone app with Whisper STT + GPT-5 Mini + AVSpeechSynthesizer TTS for "passenger seat AI." 2 weeks before the IRL Lore PRD.

TL;DR

  • Pitch: "AI driving companion app for iPhone that makes solo driving feel like you have a real person in the passenger seat. Think of it as your personal co-pilot who's always down to chat."
  • Cost discipline upfront: TTS would be 80% of costs at scale → use iOS native AVSpeechSynthesizer (free) instead of OpenAI TTS. Move primary STT to Whisper on-device (WhisperKit / whisper.cpp → Core ML).
  • Architecture flow: User speaks → Cloud STT (Whisper Realtime over WebSocket, streaming partials) → GPT-5 Mini for short casual 1-3 sentence responses → AVSpeechSynthesizer locally → output. Latency target: <1.5s end-of-user-speech to start-of-AI-speech.
  • UX states: Idle → Listen (hot mic / press-to-talk, animated waveform) → Think (subtle "looking up" animation, single chime, fade) → Speak (punctuation-tuned pauses, varying sentence length) → Interrupt (voice onset detection + .stopSpeaking(at: .immediate) + Whisper takes over, "no awkward fade").
  • Direct realization of the CarBUDDY idea — "talk to AI that curates and has good convos with you while you drive" — written 8 months later as a full PRD.

Pattern observed

This doc is the best surviving "stealth app PRD" in the corpus alongside IRL Lore. Both written within 2 weeks of each other in August 2025 — pre-Austin, pre-Rankett-pivot. Yahir was clearly PRD-shopping: building out 2-3 specific app concepts in detail before deciding which to ship.

Key product-thinking patterns:

  1. Cost engineering FIRST (TTS is 80% of cost → use free iOS native instead). Same discipline as Rankett's tier margin analysis.
  2. Latency-as-product (<1.5s = the experience). Same UX-rigor as IRL Lore's "<60 second first entry."
  3. Interruption handling as a feature, not afterthought — "feel like cutting off a human, no awkward fade."

The fact that neither IRL Lore nor AI Traffic Companion appears to have shipped by April 2026 suggests both got shelved when Rankett absorbed Yahir's bandwidth in late 2025.

Open questions

  • Did any prototype get built? (No SwiftUI code surfaces in the corpus.)
  • Why did Yahir choose Rankett over CarBUDDY/IRL Lore? (Speculation: Rankett had agency-side B2B revenue path; consumer apps need network effects.)
  • Was this idea triggered by Tesla FSD, ChatGPT advanced voice mode, or general "passenger seat AI" zeitgeist?
